heroHeadline: RSF General Defects to SAF in Desert Crossing; Sudan Healthcare System Collapse Reaches 2,052 Killed in Attacks
heroSubtitle: High-ranking RSF General Al Nour Ahmed Adam defected to the Sudanese Armed Forces on April 20 after fleeing RSF detention in North Darfur with 50 fighters — the second major RSF-to-SAF defection in two years, suggesting fractures in RSF command cohesion as the war enters its fourth year; WHO data now records 2,052 people killed in 217+ healthcare attacks, as Al Jazeera reported from Sudan's only functioning tropical diseases hospital in Omdurman; the conflict continues with drone strikes killing hundreds monthly and no ceasefire in sight
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle
